story.asA: developer integrating with the MCP API
story.iWant: clear documentation of the new resultMode parameter and response schemas
story.soThat: I can choose the appropriate mode for my use case
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Update OpenAPI Specification for resultMode Parameter (8 subtasks) - Review current OpenAPI spec, add resultMode parameter with enum values - Set default to "snippets", document error responses - Add mode field to response schema
### Task 2: Document Response Schemas for Each Mode (13 subtasks) - Add MinimalResult schema with all base metadata fields - Add SnippetResult schema extending MinimalResult - Add FullResult schema with gitingest_summary and repository_stats - Add example responses for each mode
### Task 3: Create Integration Examples (12 subtasks) - Create api/examples/result-modes/ directory - Create cURL examples: minimal-mode.sh, snippets-mode.sh, full-mode.sh - Create TypeScript examples: minimal-mode.ts, snippets-mode.ts, full-mode.ts - Create Python examples: minimal_mode.py, snippets_mode.py, full_mode.py
### Task 4: Create Mode Selection Usage Guide (6 subtasks) - Create api/docs/result-modes.md usage guide - Document when to use each mode, performance characteristics table - Add decision tree for mode selection
### Task 5: Update MCP Configuration Guide (5 subtasks) - Review existing Claude Desktop configuration guide - Add result modes section with examples for each mode - Document performance expectations
### Task 6: Create Migration Guide (5 subtasks) - Create api/docs/migration-result-modes.md - Document backward compatibility guarantee - Provide optimization guidance for switching modes
### Task 7: Update README.md with Result Modes Overview (5 subtasks) - Add "Result Modes" section to main README.md - Provide quick overview of three modes - Link to detailed documentation
### Task 8: Validate Swagger UI Rendering (7 subtasks) - Start local API server, access Swagger UI at /docs - Verify resultMode parameter renders with enum dropdown - Test example responses
### Task 9: Create Performance Documentation (5 subtasks) - Create api/docs/performance.md - Document performance targets for each mode - Add optimization strategies
### Task 10: Review and Validate All Documentation (7 subtasks) - Spell check, validate code examples - Check internal links, verify consistency - Validate OpenAPI spec with validator tool

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-1: OpenAPI Specification Updates - /mcp/search endpoint documents resultMode parameter with enum values - Request schema shows enum values with descriptions - Response schemas defined for MinimalResult, SnippetResult, FullResult - Examples provided for each mode
AC-2: Integration Documentation - Clear explanation of when to use each mode - Performance characteristics table (latency, bandwidth) - Code examples for TypeScript/JavaScript clients - MCP configuration examples for Claude Desktop - Migration guide for existing clients
AC-3: API Documentation Completeness - Swagger UI at /docs renders updated spec correctly - Example responses show realistic data for each mode - Error scenarios documented (invalid mode values) - Performance table from PRD included (minimal <500ms, snippets <1500ms, full <3000ms) - Backward compatibility guarantee documented

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification - MCP API Result Modes
doc.snippet: Defines three result modes (minimal, snippets, full) with performance targets, data models (MinimalResult, SnippetResult, FullResult interfaces), API contracts, and acceptance criteria AC-5 for documentation completeness

doc.title: PRD Enhancement: MCP API Result Modes
doc.snippet: Defines motivation, three result modes with examples, performance characteristics table (minimal <500ms ~1KB, snippets <1500ms ~5KB, full <3000ms ~50KB), client integration patterns, and acceptance criteria AC-5 for documentation

doc.title: OpenAPI 3.0 Specification (Current)
doc.section: Existing API schema with partial result modes implementation
doc.snippet: Already includes resultMode parameter with enum (minimal/snippets/full), SearchRequest/SearchResponse schemas, SnippetResult schema (95 lines), FullResult schema (164 lines), MinimalResult schema (partial). Stories 8.1-8.4 completed schema updates.

doc.title: MCP TypeScript Type Definitions
doc.snippet: Defines ResultMode type ('minimal'|'snippets'|'full'), MCPSearchRequest with resultMode parameter, MinimalResult interface (9 fields), SnippetResult extends MinimalResult (+6 fields), FullResult extends SnippetResult (+gitingest_summary, repository_stats, dependencies)

doc.title: Story 8.1 - Parameter Schema (Completed)
doc.snippet: Established resultMode parameter validation, enum with description, INVALID_RESULT_MODE error code with allowed_values field, backward compatibility with default parameter value

doc.title: Story 8.2 - Minimal Mode (Completed)
doc.snippet: 9 base fields (repo_url, repo_org, repo_name, language, last_updated, similarity_score, github_link, metadata), performance <500ms p95 ~1KB per result, use case: fast browsing and metadata exploration

doc.title: Story 8.3 - Snippets Mode Default (Completed)
doc.section: SnippetResult schema and OpenAPI documentation pattern
doc.snippet: MinimalResult + 6 snippet fields (snippet, snippet_file_path, snippet_line_range, context_lines_before/after, codespaces_link), default behavior when resultMode omitted, performance <1500ms p95 ~5KB, OpenAPI schema 95 lines with comprehensive field descriptions

doc.title: Story 8.4 - Full Mode (Completed)
doc.snippet: SnippetResult + 5 full-mode fields (gitingest_summary, full_file_context, readme_excerpt, repository_stats, dependencies), RepositoryStats interface (contributors, commits_last_month, open_issues, last_commit), Dependency interface (name, version, type), performance <3000ms p95 ~50KB, OpenAPI 164 lines added

doc.title: API README (Current)
doc.snippet: Overview, architecture diagram, prerequisites, environment variables, local development, deployment instructions. Needs "Result Modes" section added per Task 7.

artifact.reason: Source of truth for TypeScript type definitions - use for OpenAPI schema validation and documentation examples
Current OpenAPI spec with result modes schemas from Stories 8.1-8.4 - needs final validation and example enhancements for Story 8.5
Search service implementation - reference for understanding data flow and performance characteristics
GCS integration for full mode - reference for understanding gitingest summary fetching

story-context.constraints: - Documentation-only story - NO code implementation - All formatter implementations (Stories 8.1-8.4) already complete - OpenAPI spec must validate with OpenAPI 3.0 validator - Code examples must be executable and tested - Markdown follows GitHub-flavored Markdown - Links use relative paths for internal documentation - Examples use realistic data from govreposcrape domain (UK government repositories) - Maintain consistency with existing docs/ directory structure - Follow OpenAPI 3.0 schema definition patterns from Story 5.2 - Reference backward compatibility patterns from Epic 7 migration docs - Examples should use alphagov/govuk-frontend and HMRC repositories for realism

interface.name: POST /mcp/search
interface.signature: Request: { query: string, limit?: number, resultMode?: 'minimal'|'snippets'|'full' } Response: { results: MinimalResult[]|SnippetResult[]|FullResult[], metadata: {...}, mode: ResultMode }

interface.name: MinimalResult
interface.signature: 9 base fields: repo_url, repo_org, repo_name, language, last_updated, similarity_score, github_link, metadata

interface.name: SnippetResult
interface.signature: MinimalResult + 6 snippet fields: snippet, snippet_file_path, snippet_line_range, context_lines_before, context_lines_after, codespaces_link

interface.name: FullResult
interface.signature: SnippetResult + 5 full-mode fields: gitingest_summary, full_file_context, readme_excerpt, repository_stats, dependencies

tests.standards: - Vitest testing framework for unit tests - Integration tests for end-to-end API calls - TypeScript strict mode compliance required - Test coverage target: 80%+ for new code - Documentation validation: spell check, link checking, OpenAPI validator - Code examples must be executable (can run curl/TypeScript/Python examples) - Realistic test data from UK government repositories (alphagov, HMRC)
tests.locations: - test/ - Integration tests - api/examples/ - Executable example code (to be created) - api/docs/ - Documentation files (to be created)
tests.ideas:
AC-1: OpenAPI Specification Updates - Validate OpenAPI spec syntax with OpenAPI 3.0 validator tool - Verify resultMode parameter enum values match TypeScript types - Check response schema definitions for MinimalResult, SnippetResult, FullResult completeness - Validate example responses use realistic UK government repository data
AC-2: Integration Documentation - Verify all code examples (cURL, TypeScript, Python) are executable - Test MCP configuration examples work with Claude Desktop - Validate performance characteristics table matches tech spec targets - Check migration guide confirms backward compatibility
AC-3: API Documentation Completeness - Manual test: Start server, access Swagger UI at /docs - Verify resultMode parameter renders with enum dropdown - Test example responses in Swagger UI - Validate error response schemas render correctly - Check all internal documentation links resolve correctly
